text.title: VR Design Patterns for Accessibility and Comfort Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the VR Design Patterns for Accessibility and Comfort methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Snap turning (15°-45° increments) is the default; smooth turning is opt-in.
rule.rationale: Smooth turn is the leading nausea cause.

rule.statement: Edge vignette / tunneling is on by default during locomotion; opt-out only.
At least two locomotion modes available (teleport default + 1 alternate).
Hand-tracking modes ship a controller fallback; controller-only modes ship hand-tracking where the runtime supports it.
rule.rationale: Motor differences and tracking failures need alternates.

rule.statement: A retreat-to-safe-scene control is persistently reachable within 2 inputs.
rule.rationale: Anxiety / overload exits are a right.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-vr-design-patterns.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
